review_text: Not much diffence among different brands. I got these 3700-sized waterproof tackle boxes to supplement several similar boxes made by Plano. Other than the color of the seal and labeling, I can't tell any differences between these and the Plano equivalents. Functionally, they both seal well. These boxes include two small plastic boxes that fit between two of the long dividers (see attached photo). These small boxes may come in handle for storing small swivels or hooks, but insignificant for choosing one brand over the other in my opinion. The Plano boxes generally have slightly higher listing prices, but most of the time, you can catch discounts that make prices for the Planos lower than these. I would just buy whichever is cheaper at the time of purchase.

No one knows that its a secret compartment!. .... No one knows that its a secret compartment!.. Apparently all bikes (cruisers) back in the day used to have something similar. It could be softer though
Five Stars. Nothing but the best from Yeti

The Medicine Arm. My compression arm sleeves are great. Having an aching arm, that happen during a snowy day,falling on the ice. As soon as the arm compression was put on, the pain had vanish like a floating cloud. Years of a sore arm, using up pain killers and muscles rub; now the pockets look a little chubby.

Quality product. Didn't work for what I intended but they are qua none the less. I had gotten a similar item elsewhere and I was able to remove the clip an dude the green velcro loop for other things. These have clip seen in so it can't come out. That's great if yours buying this for its obvious uses, but not so great if you wanted it for parts. The main reason even included this, is cause I hate when people give something 2-3 stars cause it didn't work for some oddball purpose the had in mind.

Amazing transformation!. Our 10' unlit tree was transformed into a beautiful snow covered giant. It does require patience and lots of water to set the flakes, but I couldn't be happier with it. I will update with pictures after I pull tree out from storage this winter if it has yellowed or fallen off more than expected, but I will probably be doing all my decor with this going forward.

Weak seat. What a shame, use that seat for the first time in the beach snd it broke. the legs are very weak, I ended up sitting on the sand. This chair is for children. It does not support the weight of an adult. What a disappointment.

Will be able to take some swings when the wind and weather is not nice outside. Come spring and summer. I recently set up for the first time. I was not hard even if I did it in the garage. Come spring and summer, it will be use outside in the back yard.

Perfect Sewing Kit!. Some evil porch pirate swiped my previous sewing kit (also this style tackle box) during a recent move. I tried several cheaper replacement options but wasn't happy. This box has all the room for tiny notions, elastics, and tools I need for my sewing projects. I only have one of my grandmother's thimbles that was elsewhere during the theft. A proper Irish curse on all porch pirates!
stands out but not overbearing. Excellent .

My go to for Dh to xc bike cleaning.. This crap works!!! No joke i put alot on and scrub it with a dish brush for about 2 min no longer as it can cause paint damage clean with water and pow cassette looks new !

Not sure what the problem is. At times it is quite difficult to take a pull from the mouth piece - as others have noted. Sometimes I un-tilt the HydroFlask and it fixes itself. The kids are still noisy with the sticks on the floor, but nothing is likely to get broken, and I seriously doubt these will send anyone to the dentist or ER.

Check the U Bolts. Check the U-Bolt tightness regularly or else you lose them. They will vibrate the nuts loose.

I hate packaging and labels that are hard to get off .... Works well, but the sticker on it won't come off without leaving a mess. Consider an alternative way of marking your product. I hate packaging and labels that are hard to get off and leave a sticky residue.

Great little flashlight and the noise of this is enough to scare people/animals away.. This is very effective on annoying people that snore. I'm kidding of course, but it is tempting to use in that manner, but I've never actually needed to use it other than the flashlight which is a decent little light. I'm not sure it would even be that painful, but the noise alone is startling and scary. I go walking in the evenings when it's dark and carry this with me, mostly for the flashlight and the noise scares charging dogs. I will warn you, don't try to travel through the Canadian Border crossing with this. They are illegal up there and will be confiscated. Same with pepper spray.

Very bad….. update: very good. UPDATE: customer service reached out and offered to make it right. After receiving another bottle, it works great, it's a little hard to get the water out at first sip but then it's normal flow. This one actually works and I can't actually get water out of it! So if I could give 0 stars I would. I literally ended up just throwing it away because I was done with it. When I order anything I spend hours comparing reviews, etc. as I did with this one. I was expecting it to be difficult to drink through the straw based on reviews……..but I got NO water through, my husband and I spent 30 minutes each trying to draw water up. No leaks, but awkward to temporarily place when partially used.. Works. Seems sturdy. Possible cons: -noisy to expand/shrink -strange looking -not sure about durability, due to constantly bending plastic when expanding -stiff when expanded

Good customer service. Update on below review. I was able to find a customer service email, and they were extremely helpful, Replaced my board and now it is awesome. I would recommend watching a video about how to blow it up to avoid the problems I had . I loved this product when I first blew It up and used it for the first time. But then went back to the lake two weeks later and it had lost air pressure, so blew it up again, and then found out that the valve is slowly leaking air. So I was going to contact the customer service at pyleUSA.com as recommended in the packaging, but by Norton's says it is an unsafe website, now not sure how to contact manufacturer, as I don't want my computer to get infected with something. Anybody have any ideas? Today, after researching and viewing utube video. I realize the my SUP is missing the Valve that is supposed to be in the air opening. Where I put air in, it is just the big hole. there is nothing there to stop the air from flowing back out like there is in the videos I watched. Still unable to contact company, as the website is considered "DANGEROUS"

My method to stop leaking. The reviewers are right. It leaks.. The item comes with 4 gaskets. One in the big lid, one in the spout fixture, one in the cover where the spout screws on, and one extra for the big lid. I doubled up the gaskets. Two for the big lid and two for the spout fixture. I removed the small gasket from the small cap. So because I don't have strong arms or big hands this is working. No leaking. It seems like the manufacturer should address the leaking problem.

Good Enough to Function For Years, at a Replaceable Price!. Quality produce for an inexpensive price! They're well made (for the price) - solid plastic injection hardware (prob not reinforced, def not ITW grade, but also not junk), webbing is a step above the cheapest grade and does not slip in HW, elastic bungie cord is the weakest link and I suspect will be the first aspect to fail after much use, stitching isn't the greatest but all the important points are double bar-tacked for reinforcement. Not a fan of the metal clips as they aren't the most compatible but what do you expect for $8ea? These aren't made to last a lifetime but they should last years! At this price, you can't cry if it fails and if your life isn't depending upon it, it's not much of a risk to take. Buy em, try em and you'll happily wind up throwing them on your cheapest rigs.
Not good for. Beatiful, Not good for boat
then you realize how solid this bad boy is. Very impressed with the quality and Heft of this stand, seems overpriced until you get it out of the box, then you realize how solid this bad boy is.
I don't recommend running in it. Works really well just difficult to get off. I don't recommend running in it.

Pretty Good Socks + a Little Fix. I like these socks. I bought some that looked pretty much the same for 38 bucks, but I didn't feel any heat at all, they sucked. Same thing now selling for $28, I wonder why. I returned them. These were 55 bucks. The amount of heat isn't super-impressive, but it makes a big difference between having cold feet and comfortable feet on those winter hikes in the woods. Which I do every day now. The COVID thing. So I charge'em the socks up every night, and wear 'em every day. A little problem, when you're carrying the socks around the house, the batteries are always falling out, banging around, and putting little strains or the cords. I don't like that, that's one way things like that fail. So I put a little safety pin in so I have to stretch the fabric slightly to get the battery out for charging. Problem solved.

There are no signs of any holes that I can patch. Had to wake up multiple times at night to inflate it. Better quality control is needed. There won't be a third time, I promise.

They popped half hour later. At first I was impressed of how many things I received and was excited to use them for my daughter's first birthday. The balloon pump was great and definitely will be using it in the future, it was easy to use. My only problem and the reason why I'm not giving it five stars is because of the balloons. After we had most of the balloons done and used what we needed, they start to pop out of no where. It started after half an hour and they just randomly popped… I prepared it a night before to have everything ready the next day and guess what?! They were popping in the middle of the night, literally my husband had to pop the rest of them quietly so it won't wake our daughter while sleeping and scare her and so the neighbors don't think we are having a shooting in our apartment… it was ridiculous. And after all that mess we had another pack of the balloons so my husband did them the day of the party and I guess the other bag was a better quality cause they didn't pop as quickly.

Just not a very good line. I'm still on the lookout for a decent, cheap fly line. This isn't it. I tested on a rod I cast constantly, comparing it to the line I typically use (a Fenwick) and fond this to be a poor performer at every distance, and on every task I put it to. I can't tell for sure just why it performs so badly...possibly the front taper is "wrong", or the weight of the line doesn't come close to the AFFTA standard? I expect Chinese lines will someday rival American-made lines, but it looks like that will take a lot longer than I had hoped. I'll test it on another rod to see if it was a mismatch, but on a rod that I know to be superb, it should have cast better than it did.

They work, with some effort and care.
Get what you paid for.. The seat works I like the shoulder strap but I wish I would have paid 10$ more for a better one. It's very light so that's a positive.

Its not reliable.. The main thing for a laserfinder has to be quickness and accuracy. This has neither. It picks up everything around the pin and is unreliable with yardage. My buddy has a Bushnell and when I compared my yardage to his it was accurate about 1 of every 4 times. My recommendation is to pay more because in this product field the more you pay the better the product. Not true for all products but for this one it is true. Do not buy this product.

These came so so late!!!. These look pretty good I just am holding back all my love for these because they came so late, I missed my opportunity to use them. Very nice rod. Poorly made reel.. I'm a fairly experienced fisherman. Trips to Canada, etc. I was excited to take my 4 year old granddaughter fishing for the first time and decided to string up the outfit before I left the house. Unfortunately, I was unable to use this outfit because the line would not release from the reel. It was caught under the gear. I took the reel apart to get it unstuck by loosening the main set screw when the thing just collapsed in my hands. I got it put together, but it still would't release any line. Safe to say, it was very cheaply made. I didn't expect much at this cost, but I thought it would get us through our first day of fishing. I'm tossing the reel, but will keep the rod. I'll find another lightweight spin casting reel to replace it. I don't advise the purchase of this item. This is not a typical Zebco product.
I'd love it but.... Came defective. The dust cap is not at all connected to the tube. Definitely seems it'll keep my water cold as it should but would love a replacement.

My Wife Did Not Like for Little Ones--Caps Tough/Tricky. The headline hits at the issue. We have 3 kids--ages 7, 7, and 8. The caps on these bottles were just too tricky. By the time they put enough force on the caps to pop them off, they's be spilling the water all over the place. We ended up going with Camelbak Chute bottles, and we LOVE THEM! Check out those. Sure, more expensive, but much better.

Will not last / waste of money. I like almost everything about this watch except that one would expect for it to last longer than 15 months. The "hook" that attaches the wristband broke off and cannot be attached back. I realized that many people have this problem often only after 7 months (and with other Fitbit models as well). I had other smart watches for much less money in the past and they lasted longer. They had other issues (charging, skin sensitivity) but spending more money on Fitbit clearly didn't solve these problem just replaced them. I would never buy Fitbit again.

Not worth the money. Bought this for a backpacking trip to Italy thinking it would be great that it folded down however it folded whenever it wanted, the mouthpiece is to small to comfortable drink from it is not worth the money!

Please don't waste your money. Takes forever to inflate and deflates slowly throughout the night. The comfort coil technology is a scheme. This bed is no better for comfort than anything else on the market.

started great, quickly failed. Update: This light sucks when you compare the cost versus value. After 2 months, it became very difficult to turn on / off. I contacted customer support and was told the ball that rests in the switch often comes off its track. This is a known problem and the only solution is to try and realign the ball within the track. It can be realigned, but only works for a couple of pushes they fall back out of place. Oh, and the lifetime warranty works if you want to pay and have it shipped back to Cateye, meanwhile you're without a light for 2-4 weeks. What is the point? Below you will see my original review touting the amazing amount of light. Any amount of light doesn't matter if the device leaves you in the dark due to a faulty switch. If you need a good light, this is what you want. This light has a total of five settings: strobe only, low-light with strobe, low light, medium light and high light. The high beam offers 1600 lumens and has great light dispersion. A neat feature, three taps of the power when on high will lower you to a lower light output. Then, two taps on the power will return you to the high beam. This allows you to toggle back and forth when changing light settings, without scrolling through all 5. I found this helpful when encountering others riding towards me. Think of it like using your high beams when driving. Run time is at least as good as advertised.

Wayyyy smaller than expected.. I ordered the Large size knee wraps. I wear a men's size 9 shoe and I could not get these things fully over my feet. I tugged at and worked with one of them for a good five minutes or so, rested, tried a few minutes more, and then I decided that this was way too long to be spending trying to get one of them on. If you are extremely patient and have an hour or two to kill you may get one on; also if you have very small feet or are not fully grown these might fit you. They are clearly good quality and will do the job for a much smaller or more patient person. If you *do* have the patience to put one on, though, be prepared to cut it off. They did arrive very quickly.
Did not have TENS Unit. Just got it and did not have the TENS Unit. Ordered a replacement so hopefully turns out better the second time.
Exploded when new and can't get money back. Really cool design and easy to maneuver but it exploded after three uses of now I can't get my money back. Pretty shady

Sloppy quality of assembly. The glass edges are glued together with clear silicone and it's done sloppy as if by a 12 year old, which might actually be true since the origins of this product are unknown - there is no industrial quality packaging. Strains of silicone are found on the glass sides and the corners are done unevenly. The shipping packaging was great and the quality of the materials is good, but the assembly could certainly use more care.

Loved it until the display failed within two weeks of purchase. I loved it initially but the display stopped functioning in less than two weeks from purchase date. It would turn on with a double tap and then split and slide two different directions followed by a screen full of gibberish. The swipe and long press functionality no longer worked. Until the display failed it was a great compact fitness tracker that fit comfortably, wasn't too large, band worked well and never fell off. The Android app (Connect) could use some user-friendly updates but had all the basic functions one would expect from a fitness tracking app. For instance, the sleep tracking information provided by the Android app was severely lacking compared to either the Jawbone or Fitbit apps, but I found that the online version through Garmin actually provided more information that the app.

Very Slippery!. I was looking for a thicker mat to help ease pressure on my knees. Bought this at Costco for a great price, but it is so slippery, i had to put my socks on to get thru class! I'm returning this!

NOT SO WATERPROOF.. I first thought this was perfect. it was super cute, small, fit everything i needed. I bought this because i was running a race and knew that water was going to be involved. The zipper unzipped twice on me, both i noticed after i was drenched. So as of now my keyless car remote no longer works and my phone is not working either. Water definitely made it inside this "waterproof" pouch.

Was hoping for good luck but.... Light, comfortable, but every time I wear them Caps lose so... I was hoping for good luck charms but seems like the ear rings will be simply a fashion statement as the Caps give it up as usual
I still use it but I'd like for it to be more sturdy. It tore at the seam after one camping trip. I still use it but I'd like for it to be more sturdy.

Not Colors Indicated. I ordered red and black bags. I received blue/yellow and blue/purple bags with red and black cases for the bags. That would be fine but the bag covers have zippers. I don't want zippers denting and scratching my nice boards.

The second day we blew it up and it had two leaks around the hole where you pump it up. It steadily lost air all day so we had to return it. Luckily returns on amazon are fairly simple.

Lasted about 18 months. It works well as a small, easily maneuverable bike. The lcd counter is a bit flimsy. I broke the clip after about one month by bumping it wrong - but that isn't why I am giving it 2 stars. The seat (pictured) has completely cracked to the point it is unusable. I have weighed between 175 and 185 for the duration I have used this bike. Well below the reported 220 pound limit. I give it 2 stars instead of 1, because it was decent while it lasted.
Nice cover for cheap. Cover is nice, fits well, good price. But mine only lasted 8 months before it leaks/ falling apart.
